  "findings": [
    "Sling angle is under 30 degrees, which dramatically increases tension on the rigging components and creates an unsafe lifting configuration."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all lifting operations immediately.",
    "Reconfigure the rigging setup using longer slings or a spreader bar to achieve a safe sling angle of 45 to 60 degrees (minimum 30 degrees)."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to ASME B30.9 and OSHA 1926.251, sling angles of less than 30 degrees from the horizontal are extremely hazardous and generally prohibited because the tension in the sling legs multiplies rapidly, exceeding the rated capacity of the rigging hardware.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251(c)(5)"
  ]
}
